title: Edge Security, WAF, Hotlinking, Headers, and Failure Containment · Go Semantics Case: Origin Shield Error Containment
summary: A public distribution trusts Referer as authorization, caches private errors, lacks browser security headers, and forwards bot bursts to an expensive origin. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the origin shield error containment increment in layered edge-security and abuse-control plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defen…
objectives: Use Origin Shield, request collapsing, failover, bounded error caching, stale delivery, and load shedding without hiding recovery or serving private content.
Use geographic restrictions only with documented business, legal, accuracy, accessibility, VPN, and privacy limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Origin Shield Error Containment · Theory Origin Shield Error Contain…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 2223 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to layer application authorization, WAF, origin protection, headers, and safe error caching; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Origin Shield Error Containment contract for the supplied data.
